questions: According to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Occupational Outlook Handbook data for the 2024–34 projection period, identify the specific Computer and Information Technology occupation that lists '5 years or more' of work experience in a related occupation as a typical entry-level requirement. Compare this role to the occupation group defined as professionals who design computer applications or identify and report defects in them. For both identified groups, report the 2024 median annual pay and the total projected numeric employment change from 2024 to 2034.

Based on the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics' Occupational Outlook Handbook projections for the 2024–2034 period, identify the computer-related occupation projected to experience a 29% employment growth and the occupation projected to see a 3% decline. For both identified roles, provide the 2024 median annual pay, the typical entry-level education, the required work experience in a related occupation, and the projected numeric employment change.

According to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics' Occupational Outlook Handbook (2024-2034 projections), a specific occupational group responsible for designing applications and reporting defects has a 2024 median pay of $131,450 and a projected employment change of exactly 287,900 jobs. Identify the related 'Management' occupation, often requiring 5 years or more of experience, that shares the exact same 15% growth rate forecast as this group. For this identified management occupation, provide the following details: the 2024 median annual wage, the specific numeric employment change projected for the 2024-2034 decade, and the average number of annual openings expected.

rounds.answer_a:
The sole Computer and Information Technology occupation in the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) 2024–34 Occupational Outlook Handbook (OOH) listing “5 years or more” of related work experience as a typical entry-level requirement is Computer and Information Systems Managers [1]. This role involves overseeing an organization’s technology strategies, budgets, and team operations, starkly differing from the comparison group: Software Developers, Quality Assurance Analysts, and Testers—professionals tasked with designing custom computer applications or identifying and reporting software defects to enhance functionality [2]. For 2024 median annual pay, Computer and Information Systems Managers earn $164,070, while the comparison group’s median is $124,200 [1][2]. Regarding 2024–34 projected numeric employment change, Computer and Information Systems Managers have a +37,400 increase (5% growth, on par with the average for all occupations), while the comparison group sees a +241,500 surge (21% growth, much faster than average) [1][2]. This gap stems from soaring demand for specialized software solutions driving the comparison group’s growth, while manager roles depend on slower organizational expansion paces and the need for seasoned tech leadership [1][2].

## References
[1] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024). Computer and Information Systems Managers. Occupational Outlook Handbook. https://www.bls.gov/ooh/management/computer-and-information-systems-managers.htm
[2] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024). Software Developers, Quality Assurance Analysts, and Testers. Occupational Outlook Handbook. https://www.bls.gov/ooh/computer-and-information-technology/software-developers.htm

rounds.answer_a:
The computer-related occupation projected to have 29% employment growth (2024–2034) per U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) Occupational Outlook Handbook (OOH) is Database Administrators and Architects, while the one with a 3% decline is Computer Network Architects [1][2]. For Database Administrators and Architects: 2024 median annual pay is $112,050 [1]. Typical entry-level education is a bachelor’s degree in computer science, information systems, or a closely related field, as they require expertise in data storage, security, and retrieval to manage organizational data infrastructures [1]. Required work experience in a related occupation is usually 1–3 years, often in roles like database developer or data analyst, to build practical skills in optimizing data access and addressing system vulnerabilities [1]. Their projected numeric employment change is +38,300, fueled by soaring demand for secure, efficient data management amid the expansion of big data initiatives across healthcare, finance, and tech industries [1]. For Computer Network Architects: 2024 median annual pay is $135,590 [2]. Typical entry-level education is a bachelor’s degree in computer science, information systems, or electrical engineering, as they design and implement complex organizational network systems [2]. Required work experience in a related occupation is 5 or more years, often in roles like network engineer or systems administrator, to develop advanced technical and project management skills [2]. Their projected numeric employment change is -2,500, linked to increased adoption of cloud-based networking solutions that reduce demand for on-premises network design specialists [2].

## References
[1] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024). Database Administrators and Architects. Occupational Outlook Handbook. https://www.bls.gov/ooh/computer-and-information-technology/database-administrators-and-architects.htm
[2] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024). Computer Network Architects. Occupational Outlook Handbook. https://www.bls.gov/ooh/computer-and-information-technology/computer-network-architects.htm

rounds.answer_a:
The occupational group responsible for designing applications and reporting defects is Software Developers, Quality Assurance Analysts, and Testers [1], which has the specified 2024 median pay of $131,450, projected employment change of 287,900, and 15% growth rate. The related management occupation often requiring 5+ years of related work experience as a typical entry-level requirement and sharing the exact 15% growth rate is Computer and Information Systems Managers [2]. For this management occupation, the 2024 median annual wage is $164,070 [2]. Its projected numeric employment change for 2024–2034 is +37,400 [2]. The average number of annual openings expected for this role is approximately 41,900, combining new positions and replacements for workers leaving the occupation [2]. This management role oversees organizational tech strategies, differing from the comparison group’s focus on app design and defect reporting, but both benefit from rising tech reliance across industries.

## References
[1] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024). Software Developers, Quality Assurance Analysts, and Testers. Occupational Outlook Handbook. https://www.bls.gov/ooh/computer-and-information-technology/software-developers.htm
[2] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024). Computer and Information Systems Managers. Occupational Outlook Handbook. https://www.bls.gov/ooh/management/computer-and-information-systems-managers.htm
